tables.title: Special Circumstances Quick-Reference
rows: Bilateral chest decompression if pneumothorax; blood products
Epinephrine per PALS; blood products (whole blood or balanced pRBC/FFP/platelets)
Rescue breaths before compressions; aggressive ventilation
Standard PALS meds; hypothermia not a barrier to resuscitation
Secure airway, provide ventilation, administer naloxone
IM epinephrine + aggressive fluid resuscitation + airway
Epinephrine 0.01 mg/kg of 1:1,000 IM (min 0.1 mg, max 0.5 mg)
Identify anatomy; maintain systemic and pulmonary flow
Standard PALS meds with awareness of parallel circulation

tables.title: Epinephrine Dosing: Anaphylaxis vs Cardiac Arrest
rows: Higher concentration, smaller volume, IM route
Lower concentration, larger volume, IV/IO route

tables.title: Anaphylaxis Treatment Summary
rows: 0.01 mg/kg of 1:1,000 IM (min 0.1 mg, max 0.5 mg)
Early intubation if stridor or edema
Early — do not wait for complete obstruction
